title: fagara procera
intro: Fagara procera, also known as the Chinese prickly ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Chinese and Ayurvedic practices, due to its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Fagara procera include relief from pain, reduced inflammation, and improved digestion, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and aid in detoxification. Fagara procera is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and extracts, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
